advisor"}],"showConjunctionBetweenSections":false,"academicYearId":0,"institutionParentId":0,"instruction":null,"advisements":[],"groupId":"eba16f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":17,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The Viticulture & Enology major provides an interdisciplinary education in the biological and physical principles underlying grape and wine production as well as practical knowledge of grape growing (viticulture) and wine making (enology). This program provides the knowledge base for problem-solving and decision-making in commercial grape and wine production.


The curriculum builds upon a foundation of biology, chemistry, biochemistry and mathematics with specialized courses related to grape and wine production. To complete the program, students may choose to place particular emphasis on viticulture, enology or economics. Credit may also be earned for foreign language study and internships.

admission"}],"showConjunctionBetweenSections":true,"academicYearId":0,"institutionParentId":0,"instruction":{"type":"Conjunction","conjunction":"Or","id":"2ff53d4a-f771-4aff-c1de-08ddb3499762","selectionType":"Complete"},"advisements":[],"groupId":"12a26f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":10,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below and maintain a 3.00 GPA across the entire group:


○    Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A

○    General Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C

○    Organic Chemistry (CHE) 008A

○    Mathematics (MAT) 019A or 021A

○    Physics (PHY) 001A/B or 007A


Visit Department agreements as needed 


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

","groupId":"57a46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

At the time of TAG submission, Viticulture and Enology requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.00 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

","groupId":"6fa46f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"Career Alternatives","groupId":"509fed09-53e4-41d0-bb24-0198da5481be","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Graduates are qualified for a variety of vineyard and winery positions, including production management, quality control and research. Additionally, they may work in related fields such as pest management, nursery production and analytical services.

","groupId":"d972a40e-0a45-4697-a677-0198da5481bf","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Group 4: Please see Selective Major Requirements Admissions Information for alternative admissions MAT course options.
